All cases treated symptomatically. 02 stool samples sent to Microbiology Department, NICED Beleghata, negative for pathogens. Health education given regarding food hygiene and sanitation.

Cases reported from Village Bhubanpur, GP Gopalpur II, Block Haroa, District North 24 Parganas. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. All cases treated symptomatically. 06 rectal swab and 05 water sample sent to RG Kar Medical College Hospital; result awaited. Arrangement of alternate drinking water done. IEC done regarding personal hygiene and safe drinking water.

Cases of fever, headache and body ache reported from Village Kanora, SC Bagda, Block Puncha, District Purulia. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. All cases treated symptomatically. RDK done on 30 patients found negative for malaria. 06 serum samples sent to Bankura Medical College for Dengue NS1 ELISA; result awaited. Anti larval activities and fogging done in the affected area. Health camp organized. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases of abdominal pain, vomiting and loose motion reported from Village Kusumda, Karkai, Jamna, Block Pingla, District. West Medinipur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. All cases treated symptomatically. Probable cause of outbreak was contamination of drinking water due to pipeline leakage. 05 stool cultures show no growth of pathogens. 5 water samples sent; result awaited. Disinfection of water sources done. ORS and chlorine tablets were distributed. IEC done regarding hygiene and sanitation

Cases reported from Hapjan, Ketetong, Kakopather, NaSadiya, District Tinsukhia. RRT investigated the outbreak. Active case search done. All cases treated symptomatically. Majority of cases are adults. Out of 67 CSF samples tested at AMCH, Dibrugarh 23 cases were found positive for JE IgM ELISA. Out of 15 deaths which occurred 05 were JE positive. Fogging and anti larval control measure undertaken. Heath education done regarding personal protection.

Cases of loose motion with mild to moderate dehydration reported from CHC Akhnoor, District Jammu. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active case search done. Symptomatic treatment given .Chlorination of water sources done. ORS packets distributed. IEC done regarding personal hygiene and safe drinking water.

Cases reported from Village Chichondi Patil, SC Athavad, PHC Takli Kazi, Block Nagara, District Ahmednagar. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house fever survey done. Out of 05 serum samples tested at Sentinel center Ahmednagar, 03 tested positive for Dengue by IgM ELISA. Entomological survey done. Anti larval activities and fogging done in the affected area. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases of fever and headache reported from Dubrajpur Municipality, ward no. 7& 12, District Birbhum. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. All cases treated symptomatically. 10 blood samples sent to Burdwan Medical College; result awaited. Anti larval activities and fogging done in the affected area. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases reported from Village Chotochalk, GP Joydev, Block Illambazar, District Birbhum. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. All cases treated symptomatically. Probable cause of outbreak was contamination of drinking water. 04 rectal swabs sent to Burdwan Medical College, E.Coli isolated in all 04 samples. Chlorination of water sources done. Disinfection of surrounding done. IEC done regarding personal hygiene and safe drinking water.

Cases reported from Villages Puratangram, Sundalpur, Gohagram, Mollasarul, GP Sirorai & Puratangram, Block Galsi-I & Galsi II, District Burdwan. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. All cases treated symptomatically. 05 samples tested negative for Dengue & Chikungunya at Burdwan Medical College. Out of 59 RDT, 02 tested positive for Plasmodium vivax. Entomological survey done. Anti larval activities and fogging done in the affected area. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases reported from Village Parbatipur Daspara, GP Payradanga, Block Ranaghat-I, District Nadia. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. All cases treated symptomatically.03 samples sent to Microbiology Department, Kalyani, Nadia, tested negative for Dengue. Anti larval activities and fogging done in the affected area. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

A total of 135 cases were reported in 30th week (week ending 27th July). Further 155 cases were reported in 31st week (week ending 3rd August 2014) from Panihati Municipality area, District North 24 Parganas. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. All cases treated symptomatically. Probable cause of outbreak was contamination of drinking water due to pipeline leakage. Out of 03 rectal swabs sent to RG Kar Medical College Hospital 02 sample positive for Vibrio cholera. Pipeline leakages repaired.ORS and chlorine tablets were distributed. IEC done regarding hygiene and sanitation

A total of 28 cases were reported in 30th week (week ending 27th July). Further 42 cases were reported in 31st week ( week ending 3rd August2014 from Village Purba Chandibari, SC Chandibari, GP Deuli-I, Block Canning II, District South 24 Parganas. District RRT visited the area. A total of 03 stool samples from were collected sent to CNMC & H; result awaited. IEC done regarding personal hygiene and safe drinking water.

A total of 81 cases were reported in 30th week (week ending 27th July. Further 07 cases were reported in 31st week (week ending 3rd August 2014) from Village Tatarpara, SC Ramkrishnapur, Block Chandrakona, District. West Medinipur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. All cases treated symptomatically. 2 stool cultures sent to MMCH of which 01 sample shows growth of Eschericia coli. 5 water samples sent to PHE; result awaited. Disinfection of water sources done. ORS and chlorine tablets were distributed. IEC done regarding hygiene and sanitation

Cases of food poisoning reported from Village Inagaluru, SC Vengampalli, PHC Ananthasagaram, District Nellore. A total of 96 people consumed beef on 07/08/2014 which was 04 days old and kept without refrigeration. Out of these 14 people developed pain in abdomen and diarrhea on 08/08/2014. All the cases were treated symptomatically. Other people with history of exposure were traced and found asymptomatic. IEC was done for creating awareness regarding food safety.

Cases reported from various Subcentres of Block PHC Sonal and Block PHC Dhalai, District Cachar. A total of 93 blood smears were prepared and examined for Malaria parasite, all were negative. Seven serum samples sent to AMCH out of which 05 were positive for Japanese Encephalitis by ELISA. A fever survey was done in the area. Fogging activity was done in the area. Awareness activities were done by involving local leaders and Panchayat members. Insecticide treated bed nets were given to the people. The death occurred in a 51 year old male.

Cases of diarrhea reported from a Girl’s Hostel, Village Pratapnagar, Block Vansda, PHC Vandervela, District Navsari. Cases occurred after drinking water got contaminated due to leakage. A total of 03 stool samples sent to DPHL Navsari and 03 water samples sent to GMC Surat; Result awaited. All cases managed at OPD level and provided with ORS. Meeting was organized with hostel administration regarding prevention and control of diarrhea.

Cases of fever with rash reported from Government SSA middle school Reshi Muqam, Kandi Khas, Block Kupwara, District Kupwara. All the cases were treated symptomatically. Health education given regarding prevention and control of disease.

Cases of diarrhea reported from Taluk Chitradurga, PHC Guddadarangenhalli, Village Mallapura, District Chitradurga. RRT visited the area. A total of 02 stool samples and 04 water samples collected, stool samples were negative for pathogenic organisms and 03 water samples were non potable. Pipeline leakages were found. Contamination of drinking water with fecal matter was observed. Gram Panchayat was requested to provide alternate source of water till leakages were repaired. All the cases were treated. Health education given to maintain personnel hygiene and environmental sanitation.

Case reported from Block Vilappil, PHC Balaramapuram, Village Athiyannoor, District Trivandrum. The cases had consumed food in a religious ceremony in a temple. It was observed that water used for cleaning and food preparation was contaminated. A total of 05 water samples taken and submitted for testing at PH Lab. Blood samples were also taken for Testing Hep A and E.

Cases reported from Block Panoor, PHC / Village Kunnothuparamba, District Kannur. All the cases participated in a marriage function on 02/08/2014 and consumed Biryani and Payasam. Majority of affected were children and presented with diarrhea, vomiting and blood in stool. RRT visited the area. Health education given regarding food hygiene and sanitation. Water sample collected and sent to KWA Kannur.

Cases reported from Kalam, Moha, Boarda, Shelka Dhanora, District Osmanabad. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house fever survey done. A total of 05 serum samples taken and sent to Sentinel center Beed; result awaited. Entomological survey done. Anti larval activities and fogging done in the affected area. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases of fever with rash reported from Village Ahikaura, SC Baheri, Block Dhanapur, District Chandauli. RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. All cases belonged to 2 – 5 years age group. Symptomatic treatment given to cases. Health education given regarding isolation of cases & personal hygiene.

Cases reported from Village Keelkathirpur, PHC Thirupukkuzhi, HSC Perumbakkam, Block / District Kancheepuram. A total of 07 blood smears taken were negative for Malaria and 06 serum samples sent to KIPM; 04 positive for Chikungunya. Door to door fever surveillance done. Disinfection done in the area. Panchayat was requested to get over head tank cleaned once a week and supply chlorinated water.

Cases of diarrhea reported from Village Arthagoor, HSC Noganur, PHC Kakkadasam, Block Thally, District Krishnagiri. The outbreak occurred due to consumption of contaminated water. OHT cleaned and chlorinated. Health education given and local body requested to provide safe water.

Cases of fever reported from Village Ponnagaram and Anthoniyarpuram, PHC Ammapattinam, Block Manamelkudi, HSC Vichur, District Pudukottai. RRT investigated the outbreak. A total of 46 blood smears were prepared out of which 12 were positive for P Vivax. Vector breeding was identified in wells. Fever survey done and preventive measures undertaken.

Cases of fever reported from Village Manimangalam, HSC Melpalanandal. PHC Mangalam, Block Thurinjapuram, District Thiruvannamalai. Pit tapping and water storage habits led to mosquito breeding. Medical team deployed. House to house fever surveillance done. Source reduction done by removing stored water. A total of 10 serum samples sent to ZET Vellore; 03 were positive for chikungunya. Indoor fogging operation was carried. Regular monitoring of pipeline leakages and rectification started.

Cases of food poisoning reported from Dhanalaksmi Srinivasan College of engineering girl’s hostel, Village Sengeri, PHC Kurumbulur, Block / District Perambulur. A total of 03 water samples (02 found non potable) and 02 rectal swabs sent to DPHL Perambulur; result awaited. Disinfection of cooking room done along with chlorination of water sources. IEC done regarding safe cooking practices.

Outbreak of fever reported from Village Harpur, PO Masunda, PS Amdanga, District North 24 Parganas. 30 blood slides tested for Malaria parasite & 10 RDT done, all were negative. 43 more slides were collected; result awaited, Symptomatic treatment given.

Cases of diarrhea reported from Allalpur, Rupsons, Raina-1, District Burdwan. Block RRT investigated the outbreak. Four water samples were tested, found non potable. One rectal swab tested positive for E Coli. Halogen tablets, ORS packets distributed. Health education on hygiene & sanitation given.

Cases of diarrhea reported from Kamchey, Goghat I, District Hooghly. Block RRT investigated the outbreak. A total of 3 rectal swabs & 1 water sample sent; result awaited. ORS packets and halogen tablets distributed in the affected areas. IEC activities undertaken regarding safe food and drinking water, hygienic practices and sanitation.

Cases of diarrhea, vomiting, fever reported from Village Dhamla (Purba Maitypara, Palpara), Khanakul-I, District Hooghly. Block RRT investigated the outbreak. 1 stool sample tested negative for pathogens & 1 vomitus sample collected; result awaited. ORS packets distributed. IEC activities done.

Cases of diarrhea reported from Village , Karko, Pangkang, PHC Jengging, District Upper Siang. Cases occurred due to contamination of drinking water due to heavy rains. All cases treated. Chlorination of water tanks done. IEC done.

Cases of fever reported from Villages Ngaming, Palsi, Zido, Bona, Pango, PHC Tuting circle, District Upper Siang. A total of 41 blood smears were examined out of which 11 were P Falciparum positive, 18 for P Vivax and 12 for both. IEC done. Treatment provided to cases and LLINs distributed.

Cases of diarrhea reported from CHC Malkharoda, PHC Faguram, Section Kirari, Village Kirari, District Janjgir. Block combat team visited the area and a medical camp was organized. Chlorine tablets were provided to the people and stocked with health workers. Health education was given to people through VHNSC and health staff.

Cases of diarrhea reported from CHC Jaijaipur, PHC Thathari, Village Thathari, District Janjgir. Block combat team visited the area and a medical camp was organized there. Chlorine tablets were provided to the people and stocked with health workers. Health education was given to people through VHNSC and health staff.

Cases of diarrhea reported from Block / CHC Bagbahara, PHC Komakhan, SC Siwani, Village Siwani Khurd, District Mahasamund. Cases occurred due to consumption of contaminated water. IEC done regarding safe water and food practices.

Cases reported from CHC Nawagarh, PHC Seoni, Village Khokhara, District Janjgir. Block combat team visited the area and a medical camp was organized there. Chlorine tablets were provided to the people and stocked with health workers. Health education was given to people through VHNSC and health staff.

Cases reported from Village Wadipora, Block Chadoora Budgam, District Budgam. RRT visited the area. Medical camp organized in the area. all cases treated symptomatically and provided with ORS and Zinc tablets. A total of 03 water and 02 stool samples collected and sent to JNLM Rainawari. One water sample tested unfit for drinking while stool samples were negative for any pathogenic organisms. Health education given regarding safe drinking water and hand washing.

Cases reported from Pootharkkal, Block Cherpu, SC / Village Chevvur, District Thrissur. Index case was a patient with history of frequent travelling and eating outside food. He was treated for Hepatitis A in June. Other cases which developed jaundice were his friends and residing in neighborhood. They had history of consuming food together with index case in a party. Serum samples collected, tested positive for Hepatitis A. Chlorination done in the area. IEC done in the area and all preventive measures were taken.

Cases of fever with rash, joint pains, vomiting and dysentery reported from Village Parbatipur Daspara, GP Payradanga, Block Ranaghat I, District Nadia. A total of 3 samples were collected & sent to Microbiology department, JNM Medical College. All 3 samples were IgM negative for Dengue.

Cases of diarrhea reported from Gourberia, Shilberia, Ramnagar-II, Hanskhali, District Nadia. All cases had consumed food (dal, alu, chokha, beef preparation, rice, curd, rasgolla) at a feast after which cases suffered from vomiting followed by loose motions on the same day. Block Medical team investigated the outbreak. One vomitus sample & one water sample were collected & sent to NICED, Beleghata. Ecoli and S Auresus was isolated in Beef, Curd and rasgolla. Water sample tested positive for coliform (E Coli). No pathogen was detected in vomitus sample. 4 food samples were collected & sent to State Food Analyst lab out of which 3 samples were found unsafe for consumption. All cases treated.

Cases of diarrhea, pain abdomen and vomiting reported from Harindanga, Nowapara I, Krishnagar-II, Krishnagar Sadar, District Nadia. Block Medical Team investigated the outbreak. All cases had consumed common food at a marriage ceremony. 3 rectal swab samples were collected & sent to NICED, Kolkata, all were negative for Enteric pathogens. Symptomatic treatment given. ORS distributed. Health education on personal hygiene & food hygiene given.

Outbreak reported from Panihati Municipal Area, District North 24 Parganas. 3 rectal swabs were collected & sent to RG Kar MCH out of which 2 were found positive for Vibrio Cholerae. 2 water samples were positive for E Coli. ORS packets, Halogen tablets distributed. Medical camp was organized. All cases treated.

Cases of fever reported from Village & SC Hatia, Block Labpur, District Birbhum. There was very poor water and sanitary conditions in the affected area. 8 blood samples collected & sent to BMCH Burdwan, all were negative for Dengue, Chikungunya & JE.

Cases of acute encephalitis reported from District Jalpaiguri. RRT investigated the outbreak. Pig rearing is seen in affected areas. A total of 469 serum samples tested by Mac ELISA out of which 97 were positive. Contact survey done in positive areas. IEC done. Intersectoral meetings arranged. Workshop on JE management done. Fogging started at affected blocks. Entomological survey done.

Outbreak reported from Sarada Mission School, Sukanta Sarani, Ward No. 7 & 10,Block Tamluk Anantpur II GP, District Purba Medinipur. District & Block RRT investigated the outbreak. 5 stool samples,1 food sample & 5 water samples were collected sent to STM Kolkata; result awaited. ORS distributed. IEC done.

Outbreak reported from Village,SC Tetulberia, GP Kumarjole, Block Minakhan, Bashihirat, Dsitrict North 24 Parganas. District RRT investigated the outbreak. 7 rectal swab samples were collected & sent to NICED Kolkata; result awaited. Water sample negative. Medical camp organized. ORS distributed. IEC done regarding personal hygiene & sanitation.

This outbreak was reported in 30th week from PHC Tamulpur, PHC Mushalpu, PHC Nizkaurbaha, PHC Barama, PHC Golagaon, PHC Jalah District Baksa. All age groups were affected. A total of 36 CSF samples were tested at GMCH Guwahati, out of which 17 tested positive for JE. District RRT visited the affected area. Fogging done in affected area. Awareness meeting done in affected areas. Indoor residual spraying done in all households.

One new case each was reported from District Bharuch, Gandhinagar and 01 from District Kheda in 32nd week. serum samples from all new cases is sent to NIV Pune; result awaited. Insecticidal dusting (Malathion 5%) have been carried out on loose soil, cracks / crevices in and around houses as well as cattle sheds. Invovlement of animal husbandry department for collection of animals samples. Group discussions were organized to strengthen community awareness regarding sanitation and personal protection measures especially in pediatric age group.

District RRT visited the area. All 12 serum samples sent to HIHT Jollygrant, Dehradoon out of which 09 were positive for Measles by IgM ELISA. IEC done and Vitamin A given for Prophylaxis.

Cases of vomiting and loose Motion reported from Village Pallamalli- Christian palem, SC Pallamalli PHC Bandlamudi, Block Chimakuthy, District Prakasam. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. All cases treated symptomatically. Cases had consumed common food in a social gathering (paysam). 01 water samples sent to District Public Health Lab; result awaited. 01 food sample sent; result awaited. Chlorination of water sources done. Chlorine tablets and ORS packets distributed. Health education given regarding food hygiene and sanitation.

Cases of vomiting and abdominal pain reported from Girls Hostel, Urban, Rajyalakshmi Colony, PHC / Block Markapur, District Prakasam. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. All cases treated symptomatically. Cases had consumed common food ( rice & dal). 01 water samples sent to District Public Health Lab, Ongole, found non potable. Chlorine tablets and ORS packets distributed. Health education given regarding food hygiene and sanitation.

Cases of vomiting and diarrhoea reported from Village/SC Malharia,ward no.12, PHC Baisee, District Purnia. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Chlorination of water sources done. Disinfection of surrounding done. IEC done regarding personal hygiene and safe drinking water.

Case with high fever reported from Village Devala (Uparkot Vistar), PHC Jira (Dhari), Block Dhari, District Amreli. District RRT visited the area. Out of 6 blood samples collected and sent to suri sardar hospital for lab investigation 1 is Dengue IgM ELISA positive and two samples are equivocal of dengue. Entomological survey & Fever surveillance done. Anti larval activities and fogging done in the affected area. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases reported from Village Na lhati Municipality (ward no.12 &15), SC Pahari- Natungram, Block Birbhum , District Birbhum . District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active case search done. All cases treated symptomatically. Poor sanitation was observed in the community. Out of 6 blood samples collected and sent to Suri sardar hospital for lab investigation 1 is Dengue IgM ELISA positive and 01 sample equivocal of dengue. Entomological survey & Fever surveillance done. Anti larval activities and fogging done in the affected area. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases reported from Village Borpasi Gaon, PHC Tipomia, Block Titabor, District Jorhat. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active survey done. All cases treated symptomatically. Out of 06 water samples tested 02 samples found non potable.02 stool samples tested at DPL found positive for E.coli. ORS and chlorine tablets distributed in the area. Health education given regarding sanitation and hygiene.

Cases of dehydration, abdominal pain and headache reported from Village Ekdarwa, Ward No 07, PHC Ekdarwa, District East Champaran. RRT visited the area. Active survey done. All cases treated symptomatically. Out 06 stool samples collected and sent to Duncan hospital.Raxual 01 sample tested positive for cholera by hanging drop. Cases reported from Village Simraha Rahika, SC Tamghati, District Araria. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. IEC done.
Cases reported from Village Amha, Block Pipra ,District Supaul. . District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Symptomatic treatment given.IEC regarding isolation of cases.

Cases reported from village Balasinor city, SC Balasinor 1-2, UHC Balasinor, Block Balasinor and District Kheda. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Cases reported from Mahidhapura UHC and DKM UHC, District Surat. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Symptomatic treatment given. Cases of diarrhea and vomiting reported from urban area of Kapas Mandi, District Sirsa. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Out of 05 water samples collected 04 samples were found non potable. Pipeline leakages were repaired. Alternate water supply was provided. ORS packets given. Health education done regarding safe drinking water and sanitation.

Cases of diarrhoea, nausea and vomiting reported from Village Mahapur, Ojhadih, Laxmandih, Madhusingha, Nayadih), CHC Sarath, District Deoghar. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. All the patients were treated symptomatically. Chlorination of water sources done. Halogen tablets & ORS packets distributed. Health education imparted regarding personal hygiene and sanitation.

Cases reported from Village Kasmar , Kanji Talab ,SC Khaparia, Kesli, CHC /Block Shahpur, District Betul. District RRT visited the area. Active survey done. Symptomatic treatment given. Out of 106 blood smears examined 39 were found positive for falciparum malaria. Entomological survey done. Source reduction and Fogging done. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases reported from various Villages of Kuntidadargaon, Devhara, Chata, Ghurghuti, SC Madanpur, Bamhani , Dhanooli, Block Ghugri, District Mandla. District RRT visited the area. Active survey done. Cases reported from Village Dhabla, SC Dhabla Dheer, Block Kalapipal, District Shajapur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Cases reported from Village Kambalpetha, SC Rompalli, PHC Tekdatala, Block Sironcha, District Dhule. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Symptomatic treatment given. A total of 12 blood smears were tested and one found positive for P Falciparum. Rapid fever survey and Vector surveillance done in the area. Health education given.

Cases reported from Village Javelibu, SC Ghotsur, PHC Kasansur, Block Etapalli, District Gadchiroli. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Source reduction and Fogging done. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases reported from Village Godalwahi, Darachi, Irupdhodari, Vadgaon, SC Godalwahi, Chavela, Sursundi, Kosmi , PHC Godalwahi, Murumgaon, Block Dhanora, District Gadchiroli. RRT visited the area. Out of 260 blood smears examined 109 smears were positive for P. falciparum. Rapid fever survey & Entomological survey done. Cases reported from Kalyan Taluka, PHC Ajade, SC Dadadi, Village Gavthan, District Thane. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. 02 water samples sent; result awaited. Chlorine tablets & ORS packets were distributed to households. Health education given regarding safe drinking water.

Cases reported from Village Talegaon, SC Talegaon, PHC Talegaon, Block Darawha, District Yavatmal District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Fever survey done. Water containers were treated with Temephos. Guppy fish were introduced. Health education given regarding hygiene and sanitation.

Cases reported from Village/ SC Chhail, PHC Kasare, Block Sakri, District Dhule. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Symptomatic treatment given to cases. . Out of 220 containers surveyed 7 found positive for Larvae. Entomological survey done. Anti larval activities and fogging done in the affected area. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases reported from village Gavthan, HSC Dadadi, PHC Ajade, Block Kalyan, District Thane. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Probable cause of outbreak was contamination of drinking water due to pipeline leakages. 2 water samples sent for testing; result awaited. ORS packets distributed. Health education done regarding safe drinking water and sanitation.

Cases reported from Village Vellakkal and Poovalmadvu, HSC Gettupatti, PHC Palayampudur, District Dharmapuri. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Symptomatic treatment given to cases. Entomological survey done. Source reduction and Fogging done. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases of intermittent high grade fever reported from Village Veenapurva, Bandipurva, SC Sirsia, Bakwa,PHC/CHC/ Block Sirsia, District Shravasti. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active survey done. All cases treated symptomatically.30 blood smears done at DMO office found negative for malaria.IEC done.

Cases of diarrhoea reported from Village Sikarpur, Gram Panchyat Hatkalna, Municipality Kalna – I and District Burdwan. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active survey done. Halogen tabs and ORS packets have been distributed in the affected area. Health education has been given on hygiene and sanitation.

Cases reported from Village Lala, CHC/Block Bilaipur,, District Hailakandi. Block RRT investigated the outbreak. Active survey done. All cases treated symptomatically. Cases reported from Village Gangadah, HSC Bankura, CHC Maheshpur, Block Maheshpur, District Pakur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active survey done. All cases treated symptomatically. Vitamin A prophylaxis given. Five serum samples taken and sent to WHO accreted lab Kolkata; result awaited. IEC done regarding isolation of cases.

Cases of fever, nausea, vomiting and headache reported from Village Maloor, HSC Tholambra, PHC Maloor, Block Iritty and District Kannur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active survey done. All cases treated symptomatically. Out of 39 serum samples sent to Regional Public Health Lab,Kannur 06 serum samples tested positive for Hepatitis A.

Cases of diarrhea, vomiting and fever reported from Village Somalwada, SHC Sankheda, Sector Pathrota, Block Kesla, District Hoshangabad. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Symptomatic treatment given. 3 stool samples collected and sent to GMC Microbiology deptartment Bhopal tested negative for Cholera. Chlorination of water sources done.ORS packets distributed. Health education done regarding safe drinking water and sanitation.

Cases reported from Village Dhabri, Block Polaykala, SC Amlay, District Shajapur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Symptomatic treatment given. A medical camp conducted by BMO with staff. 86 blood smears tested negative for malaria.IEC done.

Cases of fever with rash reported from Village Bardwaja, Chalkkolia, Barkolia, Ismalpur, Gram Panchyat Nabagram, District Howrah. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active survey done. Symptomatic treatment given. 08serum samples sent; result awaited. Vitamin A supplementation given. IEC done regarding isolation of cases.

A total of 18 cases were reported in 44th week (week ending 2nd November 2014). Further 33 cases were reported in 45th week (week ending 9th November 2014) from Deoli, Pulgaon, Gandhi ward, District Wardha. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Symptomatic treatment given to cases. Increase in mosquito breeding was observed in the area. A total of 115 blood smears were examined and all were negative for malaria. Out of 51 serum samples tested 23 samples tested positive for Dengue by ELISA. Source reduction and Fogging done. IEC done regarding personal protective measures.

A total of 32 cases reported in 44th week (week ending 2nd November 2014). Further 42 cases were reported in 45th week (week ending 9th November 2014) from Village Miapara, Gram Panchyat Nayabasti, Silampur, MunicipalityKaliachak- I, District Malda. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active survey done. Symptomatic treatment given. Out of 36 blood samples tested 10 samples tested positive for Dengue IgM ELISA. Out of 06 blood samples tested by NS1 rapid test kit, 04 samples found positive for Dengue.02 deaths occurred of which one was a 28 year old male and one was 30 year old female and both were found NS1 positive. Health education done regarding personal protective measures.

Cases of fever with rash reported from villages Tapra, Majrahi and Bisaria, HSC Birnagar, PHC Bhargama, District Araria. District and Block RRT investigated the outbreak. 5 samples were collected and sent to PMCH Patna; all are positive for measles

Cases of fever with rash reported from village Krishna nagar, Dedaur Panchayat, PHC Sadar, District Nawada. Affected age group is 11 months to 13 years. Block RRT investigated the outbreak.5 blood samples collected and sent to PMCH, Patna, all 5 were positive for Measles IgM ELISA. Symptomatic treatment given to cases. Vitamin A supplementation given.

Cases of fever with rash reported from village & SC Khutali, PHC Dudhani, Block Silvassa. Age group of the affected cases range from 1-10 years. Majority of the cases have received 1 dose of Measles vaccine. 6 serum samples collected & sent to DPHL Silvassa, out of which 3 were positive for Measles IgM ELISA. No more cases have been reported.

Cases of acute onset diarrhea and vomiting reported from S. S .Hospital, Petlad, Block Petlad, District Anand. District and Block RRT investigated the outbreak.7 water samples collected and sent to state public health lab, all were fit for consumption. Control measures undertaken.

Cases of fever with rash reported from villages Billpudi1 & 2,, PHC Sidumbar, Block Dharampur and district Valsad. District RRT investigated the outbreak.5 serum samples sent for testing to BJMC Ahmedabad, all were positive for Measles IgM ELISA. , Treatment provided to cases. House to house survey done.

Cases of fever with vomiting reported from Village Muchoradaldali, SC Badore, Block Boarijore, District Godda. RRT investigated the outbreak. Out of 59 slides examined, 11 were positive for Falciparum malaria. Health education given regarding use of mosquito nets. Symptomatic treatment given to al the cases.

Cases of fever with rash reported from Village Sarvil, SC Maudi, Block Barajamda, District West Singhbum. District & Block Health Team investigated the outbreak. Majority of the cases were unimmunized. 5 serum samples were collected & sent to Institute of Serology, Kolkata; out of which 4 were positive for Measles IgM ELISA. House to house survey done. Health camp was organized. All cases treated symptomatically. IEC activities done. Isolation of the cases done. Vitamin A solution was given to the affected children.
Cases reported from village village Lokur, PHC/CHC U Betageri, Taluka / district Dharwad.RRT investigated the outbreak.2 serum samples collected and sent to KIMS Hubli, result awaited.

Cases reported from village Padesur, PHC/CHC Alagwadi, Taluka Navalgund district Dharwad. DistrictRRT investigated the outbreak. 2 serum samples collected and sent to KIMS Hubli,both the samples tested positive for Chikungunya IgM ELISA.

Outbreak reported from SC Sansarpur, Block Bahoriband, District Katni. All the cases started vomiting after consuming food. All of them are students of primary school Sansarpur. All cases treated at District hospital Katni.

Case of fever reported fromVillage Bhavsinghpura, PHC Jawar, Block/ District Khandwa. Rapid fever survey done. I sample tested positive for Dengue by IgM ELISA. District Malaria team visited the affected area & treated all cases. Fever survey done. Aedes larva was destroyed by temphos. Indoor survey of pyrethrum done. Health education given on personal hygiene and sanitation.
Cases reported from Village Gondi guradia, Block Budhni, District Sehore. all the cases had consumed ratanjot seeds after which they started vomiting. All cases treated. Human samples?

Cases of fever reported from Village Ghogargaon, SC Belipimpalgaon, PHC Newasa B, Block Newasa, District Ahmednagar. There is increase in mosquito density. Entomological survey was done in the affected area. HI- 4.25, BI-5.61, CI-2.60. Out of 37 serum samples tested at sentinel site Ahmednagar, 10 are positive for Dengue IgM. Rapid fever survey done. fogging done. Health education given.

3 Cases of fever reported from Village Maregaon, SC Chambhara, PHC Amirza, Block & District Gadchiroli. 01 case of fever reported from Village Regadi SC Madeamgaon, PHC Regadi, Block Chamorsi District Gadchiroli.District RRT investigated the outbreak. 72 blood smears were tested, out of which 3 were positive for PF and 1 for PV. Fever survey done. Medical Camp was organized in the area.

Cases of fever reported from Village, SC & PHC Wadner Bhairav, Block Chandwad District Nashik.District RRT investigated the outbreak. Entomological survey was done in the affected area & revealed HI-8.47, CI_5.34. 3 serum samples were tested at DPL Nashik; result awaited. 5 blood slides were tested for malarial parasite, all were negative. Fever survey done. Health education about environmental sanitation given. Indoor spray with 2% pyrethrum done.

Cases of fever reported from Village Ambad, SC & PHC Nanashi, Village & PHC Kochargaon Block Dindori, District Nashik. RRT investigated the outbreak. 12serum samples sent to DPL Nashik, all are negative for Dengue. 49 blood slides were tested for malarial parasite, all were negative. Fever survey done. Cases reported from Govt. Middle & Primary school, village Jatoli, HC Nurpur Bedi, District Rupnagar. Parents asked to keep the infected children in isolation. Health education given to school staff, students and parents regarding the disease. House to house survey done.

Cases reported from Central University Bandarasindiri, SC Bandarasindri, PHC Harmada, Block Kishangarh, District Ajmer.RRT investigated the outbreak. All 10 blood samples tested positive for Hepatitis A by IgM ELISA. 8 water samples sent for testing at DPL Ajmer, result awaited.2 food samples taken. Inspection of mess and canteen kitchen done. A Case of 7year old female child reported from village & SC Umrain, CHC Wair, Block Bhusawar, District Bharatpur. No more new cases detected. Immunization status not known.

Cases of jaundice reported from Juni Mandi, Old Jodhpur city, District Jodhpur. The outbreak is suspected to be due to pipeline leakage. All age groups affected. Out of 5 blood samples tested at Virology Lab, SMS Jaipur 2 were positive for Hepatitis A & 3 were positive for Hepatitis E. 10 water samples were collected & sent to lab for testing for fecal contamination, all were positive for fecal contamination. Door to door survey done. IEC activities done regarding early treatment of cases.

Cases of fever with joint pains reported from village & HSC Veeranam, PHC Sathanur Dam, Block Thandrampattu, District Tiruvannamalai.RRT investigated the outbreak.10 Blood smears sent to PHC all were negative. 3 serum samples were collected and sent to Zonal Entomological Team Lab Vellore, out of which 1 was positive for Dengue and Chikungunya, 1 was positive for dengue and scrub typhus. Minor ailments treated. Anti- larval and anti adult measures undertaken.

Cases of fever reported from Andhiyur, Athani, Panchampalayam, Kollapalayam & pallapalayam, District Erode. Out of 7 blood samples tested at DPHL, Erode, 4 were positive for Chikungunya on IgM ELISA. Fever survey done. source reduction done. Anti-larval activities conducted. Health education given.

Cases of fever with joint pains reported from village & HSC Thondamandurai, PHC Arumbavar, Block Veppanathattai, District Perambalur. Out of 6 serum samples tested for Dengue at DPHL Perambalur, 1 tested positive for Dengue on IgM ELISA. . Source reduction done. Antilarval and anti adult activity done.IEC done.

Cases of fever with nausea and arthalgia reported from village & HSC Naranamangalam, PHC Padalur, Block Alathur, District Perambalur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Out of 11 serum samples 6 tested positive for Typhoid by widal. Pipe leakage rectified chlorination of water and disinfection done.

Cases of fever reported from village S Vellakulam, HSC T. Velangudi, PHC Veeracholan, Block Narikudi, District Virudhnagar.District RRT investigated the outbreak. 4 serum samples were collected & sent to ZET Virudhnagar, out of which 1 was positive for Dengue IgM ELISA & 1 was positive on Widal.Fever survey done. Source reduction and antilarval activity done.

Cases of fever with joint pains reported from village & HSC Saruguvalauapatti, PHC Keelavalavoo, Block Vllalore, District Madurai.District RRT investigated the outbreak. Out of 5 serum samples tested for at DPHL Madurai Medical College, 2 tested positive for Leptospirosis and 3 tested positive for Chikungunya.. Source reduction done. Antilarval and anti adult activity done.IEC done. Chlorination of water and disinfection done.

Cases of headache and pain abdomen reported from Hayanakuppam PUM School, village Hayanakuppam, PHC Vanapuram, Block Rishivandhiyam, District Villupuram Kallakuruchi.District RRT investigated the outbreak. All children had consumed rotten egg and sambhar sadam at Mid Day Meal.1 water sample and 1 food sample tested at DPHL Kallakurichi. Water sample was found to be potable & no pathogens were found in food sample. Disinfection and cleaning of OHT done. IEC given.

Cases reported from Mohalla Balapatti, Hathras city, District Hathras. District RRT investigated the outbreak. One 07 year old male child died due to the condition.

Cases reported from Jalaberia Sahachari School, village Ratikontomore, GP Jalaberia I, Block Kultali, district South 24 Paragana. District and Block RRT investigated the outbreak. Onset of symptoms after 1 ½ hours of eating Mid Day Meal containing cooked dal with Lizard in it.2 stool samples sent to CNMCH, both samples were negative for any pathogenic organism. Active case search done.IEC done.

Cases of diarrhea reported from PHCs Katra, Sakra, Aurai and Musahari, District Muzzafarpur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. 5 samples sent to SKMCH, Muzzafarpur, result awaited... 4 positive for cholera on culture

Cases of fever with rash reported from village Inderanagar, SC Pipali, PHC Khareda, Block Morbi, District Rajkot majority of the cases are between 0-5years of age (6). .4 out of 5 serum samples sent to Medical College Rajkot tested positive for Measles IgM ELISA. House to house survey done. Treatment provided to cases.IEC activity done.

Outbreak reported from Village Kodiyathur, Block PHC Cheruvady (Kunamangalam), District Kozhikode. Source of infection is suspected to be contaminated water. District Surveillance team investigated the outbreak. 15 samples were collected & sent to Regional Public Health Lab, Kozhikode, out of which 7 tested positive for HAV IgM ELISA. House to house survey done. Mass chlorination done. Medical camp was organized. Health education given.

Cases of fever with joint pains reported from Village Umarkhaal & V Block Ichhawar, District Sehore. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Out of 23 blood samples tested at District Malaria Office Bhopal, 7 were positive for chikungunya. Larva survey done. 35 blood slides tested for malaria, all were negative for malaria. Pyrethrum & Temephos spray done. One 6 year old boy died due to the condition?

Cases of fever reported from Villages Chapan Khurd , Jivapur Mahodia & Bavra, Block Ashta, District Sehore. RRT visited the affected area along with malaria department. 52 Blood slides were tested for malarial parasite, all were negative for Dengue.

An outbreak of fever with joint pain reported from several bocks namely Lakhandur, Lakhani, Sakoli, Tumsar & Mohadi of District Bhandara. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search of fever cases done. Medical camps were organized in the community. All cases treated symptomatically.07 deaths occurred in < 45year age group. Entomological survey revealed high indices (>7)for HI, BI, CI. Fogging and source reduction were done in the community. Health education regarding personal protection given.

Cases reported from Village Bordeorigaon, SC Borderi, PHC Dhalpur, Distt Lakhimpur. The affected persons had history of consumption of rice and pork on 23/11/2014 after which they developed vomiting, pain abdomen and loose stools. All the cases were above 10 years of age. A total of 05 stool samples were collected and sent for culture and sensitivity; no pathogenic organisms were isolated. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Health education is given to the community.

Cases reported from Village Annaram, SC PHC/ Mandal Chervu Annaram, Mandal Kattangur, Distt Nalgonda. A total of 174 students consumed midday meal, after which 34 of them developed symptoms of Nausea, Vomiting, headache and stomach pain. Medical team visited the area. Health education given to the food handlers, school staff and students regarding food hygiene.

Cases reported from Village / SC Aitipamula, PHC/ Mandal Kattangur, Distt Nalgonda. A total of 131 students consumed midday meal, after which 32 of them developed symptoms of Nausea, Vomiting, headache and stomach pain. Medical team visited the area. Health education given to the food handlers, school staff and students regarding food hygiene.

Cases reported from Village/ UHC/Taluka Kalol. .District Gnadhi Nagar.District & Block RRT investigated the outbreak. . 2 blood samples taken and sent for viral hepatitis serology Both the samples found to be positive for HAV. IEC activity was done regarding hand washing and boiling of water along with regular chlorination of drinking water.

Cases reported from Village/SC Sonarda, PHC Vadodara, District Gandhinagar. District RRT investigated the outbreak. 02 stool samples sent to Civil Hospital Gandhi Nagar ,both samples were found to be negative for any pathogen. 01 water sample sent to GJTI (Gujtrat Jalseva Training Institute ); result is awaited. House to house survey done. Health education given to the people in the community.

Cases reported from Guhai Colony Himmatnagar, Taluka Himmatnagar, Distt Sabarkantha. Cases presented with pain in abdomen and vomiting. 14 serum and 02 water samples sent to Civil Hospital Himatnagar, out of 14 serum samples 07 found to be positive for HEV. Both the water samples were found to be potable. House to house survey done. Chlorine tablets were distributed and health education given.

Cases reported from Village Pilol, SC Bakrol, PHC Jantral, Taluka Kalol, Distt Panchmahal. District RRT investigated the outbreak. All the cases had history of consumption of food in a Vastu Pujan ceremony. No food/stool or water samples were sent for testing. House to house survey done to identify all the cases. Health education given to the people in the community.

Cases reported from Village Kaddiya, SC Keska, Block Tundi, Distt Dhanbad. District RRT investigated the outbreak. One male child aged 10 months & one female child aged 02yrs died due to the condition.05 blood sample taken and sent to Institute of Serology, Kolkata.All 05 samples found to be positive for Measles IgM ELISA. Immunization activities were strengthened in the area. Health education given to the people regarding isolation of cases.

Cases reported from Village Jatajhopdi, Ranidih Koketola and Bazar Tola, CHC Golmuri cum Jugsalai, Distt East Singhbhum. All the wells in the area were chlorinated. Health education was given to the people and advised to maintain hygiene and sanitary measures.

Cases reported from Village Simrakitta, SC Bhandaridih, PHC / Block Mahagama, Distt Godda. District RRT investigated the outbreak. A total of 05 serum samples were collected and sent to Institute of Serology, Kolkata for Ig M; out of which 04 were found to be positive for Measles IgM ELISA. All the cases were treated symptomatically. Immunization activities were strengthened in the area.Health awareness programme were conducted in the village.

Cases reported from Village Neema, SC Barasimra, PHC / Block Boarijore, Distt Godda.District RRT investigated the outbreak. Majority of cases were males and <01 year old.oOut f 05 serum samples collected and sent to Institute of Serology, Kolkata for IgM; 03 samples found to be positive for Measles IgM ELISA. the cases were treated symptomatically. Immunization activities were strengthened in the area. Health awareness programme was conducted in the village.

Cases reported from Village /SC Kharpose, Block Manjhgaon, District West Singhbhum. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey was done. Majority of cases belonged to 11-20 year age group. A total of 05 serum samples were collected and sent to Institute of Serology, Kolkatta for IgM all of them were found to be negative for Measles & Rubella. Health awareness programme were conducted in the village.

Cases reported from Village Sukki, SC Balasa, PHC Tadkalas, TQ Purna.District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done and mosquito larvae found during investigation. 10 samples collected , results awaited.

Cases reported from Village Mhaisal, SC Mhaisal, PHC Mhaisal, Block Miraj. House to house survey done. Isolation ward established and contact treatment given. Disinfection of water tank along with repair of damaged pipeline done. 28 stool samples sent to Govt. Medical College, Miraj. Report awaited.

Cases reported from Village Khadiyapura, SC Durkooda& Village Manpura, SC Nichli Bahrai, Block Pahadgarh District Morena. Door door rapid fever survey done. 39 blood smears examined, out of which 16 found positive for Falciparum Malaria. Focal spraying done and health education given

Cases of fever & rash reported from Village Katra- Sunehra, PHC Raksheha, Block Devendranagar District Panna . Sick children referred to District Hospital, Panna. Measures to follow-up new cases instituted.

Cases reported from Village GajiKhedi, Block Ichhawar District Sehore. Supervisor, ANM, MPW visited the area.30 blood smears were examined, all were found to be negative for malaria. Health education given.

It was observed that the drinking water was contaminated by sewage water due to leakages in water supply line. 3 Blood samples were sent to District Public Health Laboratory out which 01 was positive for HAV & 01 was positive for HEV. 5water samples were for analysis District Public Health Laboratory, report awaited. House to house survey was done in the area. Medical camp organized in the area. IEC activities done. Water supply department requested to repair pipeline.
Cases reported from Village Bulade Wala, Block Goniana, Distt Bhatinda. All the cases were treated symptomatically. Isolation of cases was done. Health education given to all the children and adults.

Cases reported from Bidesh Tilla, Champamura GP Under old Agartala Block, Distt West Tripura. All the affected persons consumed meal at a religious ceremony. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Patients admitted in IGM Hospital. All the patients have been discharged.Health camp organized.

Cases reported from Village Kaslana, SC Dewal, Block Naugaon, Distt Uttarkashi. Of all the cases only 2 had prior history of vaccination. A total of 05 serum samples were collected and sent to HIHT Jolly Grant for measles confirmation; result awaited. Vitamin A given to the cases. Health education given regarding isolation of cases.

Cases reported from Village Pathar Madhaipur, Block Old Malda, Distt Malda. Majority of cases were among migratory population. A total of 29 samples were tested by RDK out of which 12 were positive for P Falciparum and 1 for P Vivax. 21 blood smears were examined out of which 18 tested positive for P Falciparum. Health education given to people to increase awareness.

Cases reported from Village / SC Patharghata, Block Tehatta I, Distt Nadia. All the cases had history of consumption of leftover food from a marriage ceremony, after which they developed abdominal pain and vomiting followed by loose motion. One stool and one food sample collected. Stool sample sent to NICED Beleghata, Kolkata and has been found negative. Food sample sent to the Food analyst, West Bengal State Public Health Laboratory. Result awaited. Health education given.

Cases reported from Khardaha Municipality, Ward No 17 and 18 Goswamipara, Distt North 24 Parganas. The cases presented with Low grade fever, upper abdominal pain, anorexia, vomiting and yellow sclera. A total of 05 serum and 06 water samples were taken and sent to School of Tropical Medicine Kolkata; 05 serum samples were reactive for HAV IgM .In the water samples on MPN count and culture no bacterial growth detected. Authorities were instructed to clean water supply line and take repair measures. Until then people were advised to drink boiled water and provided with halogen tablets for domestic chlorination. Health education given.

Cases reported from urban slum near Rameshavar Talav, PHC Kadiyadra, Block Idar, District Sabarkantha. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Majority of cases were above 14 years of age. All 11 stool samples collected and sent to Sir Pratap General Hospital microbiology lab Himmatnagar tested negative for any pathogen. 02 water samples to Jalbhavan Laboratory Himmatnagar; both the samples were found to be non potable. House to house surveillance done. Health education given.

Cases of fever with rash reported from Village Narayanpur, Pokharia, Lokaniya and Gokhla of Block Narayanpur, Distt Jamtara. District RRT investigated the outbreak. A total of 05 serum samples sent to Institute of Serology, Kolkata and 04 were found to be positive for Measles. All cases treated symptomatically. Health education given regarding immunization and isolation of cases.

Cases of fever with rash reported from Village Kadokhera, Block Jaganthpur, HSC Manikpur, District West Singhbhum. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Majority of cases were in the age group of 6- 12 years. All cases treated symptomatically. Health education given regarding immunization and isolation of cases.

Cases reported from Murli Dhar Vyas Colony, City / District Bikaner. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases reported from Village Chadayamangalam, SC Poonkode, CHC Chadayamangalam, District Kollam. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases reported from Village Kadakkavoor, SC Keezhattingal, PHC Anjuthengu, District Thiruvananthapuram. Cases presented with symptoms of abdominal pain, vomiting and loose stools within 6 hours of consumption. Food samples were taken and sent to food safety department analytical lab.; result awaited.Cases treated symptomatically All the neighboring food establishments were also inspected. Health education given.

Cases reported from AMLP School, Village Perumkulam, PHC Keezhattingal, Block Anjuthengu, District Thiruvananthapuram. Health education was given.

Cases of fever reported from Village Pachkhedi and Majari, PHC Madhal, Block Kuhi, District Nagpur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. An increase in larval density was observed in the area. Guppy fish introduced.

Cases of fever, body ache and pustules reported from Faizul Ulum Madarsa, SC Ahmadabad, CHC Malihabad, District Lucknow. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases of fever with joint pain reported from urban area of Pasighat, Pasighat Municipality area, District East Siang. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Cases of loose motion and vomiting reported from Village Dwarka, PHC Varvala, Block Dwarka, District Jamnagar. Medical team investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Cases of fever with rash reported from Villages Kewat Tola, Belenzabad, Jhinjhi Pahari, Block Baghmara, PHC Jogta, District Dhandbad. Medical team investigated the outbreak. Cases of fever with rash reported from Village Sokorla Rajutoli, CHC Kolebira, Block Kolebira, District Simdega. Medical team investigated the outbreak. House to house fever survey done. Cases of loose motion and vomiting reported from Village Basanthpura, PHC Chillaragi, Block/District Bidar. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for cases done. Cases consumed food in a social ceremony. Health education given regarding food hygiene and sanitation.

Cases of fever with joint pain reported from Village Phansawale, SC Phansawale, PHC Hatkhamba, Block Ratnagiri, District Ratnagiri. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Cases reported from Bansjora Basti, Kali Basti No 9, Block / District Dhanbad. Cases of fever abdominal pain & jaundice reported from Ramdev colony,Chandpole area under old Jodhpur city, District Jodhpur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. All age groups were affected. Cases reported from Village Arpakkam,,Mangalam,Kalasapakkam SC Arpakkam,PHC Mangalam , Block Thurinjapuram District Tiruvannamalai.District RRT investigated the outbreak.all the cases consumed food provided by the company. 02 stool samples were sent to GTMCH, Tiruvannamalai; result awaited. Case reported from Mohalla Pattiwala, Town Kanth, District Moradabad. Case presented with fever and petechie all over the body for which she took medicine from local practioner. District RRT along with central RRT.The patient had cattle in house and was involved in caring them. The patient was admitted in serious condition in Metro Hospital Noida on 21/01/2015 and was investigated for hemorrhagic fever and tested negative for Dengue, chikungunya, Scrub Typhus, Leptospirosis, tick fever but positive for CCHF. The sample was tested at NCDC by RT PCR. The patient expired on 28/01/2015 of internal bleeding. Central RRT along with District RRT investigated the outbreak and contact tracing was done. All the contacts of the patient are put under surveillance and are presently fine. 01 contact developed fever and his sample tested positive for CCHF at NCDC New Delhi, but he did not manifest any sign of severe illness, his contacts are put under surveillance. Tick samples tested negative for CCHF at National Institute of High Security Animal Diseases Laboratory, Bhopal.

remarks: A total of 397 cases of loose stools and vomiting reported from Villages Ramnagar, Shyamnagar, Gammru, SC Dharmkot, Chola Tau, PHC Meclodganj in 19th week (week ending 13th May 2018). Further a cumulative of 498 cases were reported from 3rd May to 27th may 2018. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search of cases done. Heavy rains reported in the affected areas. The energized hand pumps were contaminated during the rains and the community consumed contaminated water. Water samples from water storage tanks (wst) from the affected villages were collected; WST Dholadhpur was not fit for consumption. Chlorine tablets, ORS packets and Zinc tablets were distributed in the community. All cases treated symptomatically. Health education given.

A total of 9 cases and 7 deaths were reported in 20th week (week ending 20th may 2018). Further a cumulative of 17 cases and 14 deaths were reported from 3rd May to 27th may 2018. Isolation wards identified and infection control protocol strengthened. Adequate PPE and other logistics ensured in the district. Burial protocol issued. Medical camps conducted in the area for disease awareness and reducing panic. NCDC team was supporting the State in outbreak containment.

Cases reported from Village Suktaguri, PHC Sipajhar. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. All cases treated symptomatically. IEC done regarding personal hygiene and sanitation.

First confirmed case of Zika virus from the State of Rajasthan (Jaipur) was reported on 21st September, 2018. A Central Team is investigating the outbreak of Zika virus disease in Jaipur. Out of 159 cases tested positive of Zika virus at SMS Medical College, Jaipur, 64 cases were pregnant women. Last case of Zika virus disease was tested and reported positive in State on 29.10.2010. State surveillance unit, IDSP, Rajasthan has been undertaking active surveillance on daily basis for suspected cases of Zika Virus Disease in affected area of Jaipur.

Cases reported from Village Geeta Colony, Bharti Colony, Bahadhur Ke road, Sabji Mandi. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Total Stool samples - 4, Positive -2 Water sample - 13 out of which 7 found bacteriological contaminated. House to House Survey Alternate water supply arranged Awareness through Pamphlets Chlorine pellets and ORS packets distributed. All cases treated symptomatically. Health education given.

Cases reported from Village nai mandi, mahtab colony, Block Gharsana. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Blood samples were taken from all febrile cases as per case definition of Dengue, of which 126 have been found positive. Aedes larval breeding was found rampantly. Lack of awareness about mosquito breeding was also there. Vector control measures were started and personal protection against mosquitoes was advised to people.

A 24yr old female from Village Chelanur, PHC Puthiyappa, Puthur, Elathur presented symptoms with fever, altered sensorium, ptosis, seizure and was admitted on 13th July 2018 in Govt Medical College, Kozhikode. Serum and CSF samples were sent to NIV Pune. The PCR sample of CSF and serum were negative for Dengue, Chikungunya, Zika, JE and West Nile Virus. The serology of serum sample was positive for West Nile Virus (IgM ELISA). The case had multiple episode of epilepsy and CSF showed increased protein and low sugar indicating tuberculosis meningitis. Case was treated with anti TB drug. The Zonal Entomology Team carried out vector survey; fogging activities were undertaken along with larvicidal spraying in the affected area. Integrated Vector Management activities were carried out in the affected area.
Cases reported from Police Welfare L.P School Tezu. District RRT investigated the outbreak. All the affected from the same class.

A Shepard by occupation presented symptoms with fever, headache, myalgia from Village Reva, SC Dharuka, PHC Ranghola, Block Umrala. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search of cases done. The index case blood sample was sent to NIV, Pune and was positive for CCHF (Ig M ELISA). Out of 30 human close contacts samples were collected and sent to NIV, Pune; 1 sample of lab technician was positive for CCHF (IgG ELISA). 40 animal, 37 tick samples were collected and sent to NIV Pune; result awaited. Contacts were traced and were monitored. All cases treated symptomatically. Anti-tick measures were carried out. Malathion and Lime dusting done in affected areas. Health education given.

A total of 1481 cases and 207 deaths due to AES reported from various villages of multiple Districts of Assam till 17th July 2019. Out of 1481 cases of AES; 406 cases were of JE and; out of 207 deaths of AES; 94 deaths were due to JE. State and Central RRT investigated the outbreak. Majorly affected Districts were District AES JE Cases Deaths Cases Deaths Barpeta 43 11 10 5 Darrang 49 13 20 6 Dibrugarh 227 25 26 9 Jorhat 82 17 25 8 Kamrup R 45 16 27 11 Lakhimpur 105 11 35 5 Nalbari 25 9 17 5 Sonitpur 116 10 26 1 Extensive vector control measures were taken. Fogging done. Insecticide treated bed nets distributed. Health education done.

Cases of AES reported from various villages of multiple districts of Assam. State and Central level RRT investigated the outbreak. A total of 5087 blood samples were tested for JE of which 521 were tested positive. A total of 2044319 blood smears were examined of which 272 were positive for P Falciparum, 156 for P Vivax and 3 for both. Of all 257 deaths 118 were due to Japanese Encephalitis. Extensive vector control measures were taken. Fogging done. Various RRTs constituted. Insecticide treated bed nets distributed.

remarks: Cases reported from PHC/Block Jamalpur and Sadar. District RRT investigated the outbreak. 2 cases were admitted in JLNMCH, Bhaglpur and one death occurred on 24th June 2019. House to house fever survey done. Health education given.

A total of 28 cases and 1 death was reported from Village/SC Shahpur, PHC Noorpur, Block Ambala. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Further 25 cases were reported in the 40th week. remarks: Cases reported from Village Shilliang Jashar, CHC Pyursla. Block Pynursla. Both genders are affected with females being affected more (60%). All age groups affected (youngest being 8 years of age and oldest being 50 years of age) The most common symptoms exhibited by all cases are fever of unknown origin. An eschar mark was found in one case. There were two deaths, One 50 year, female with symptoms of fever, chills and nausea who expired on 9/11/2020 and a 11 year old male with symptoms of fever, chills and nausea who expired on 7/11/2020. Rapid Antigen test for COVID-19 were conducted and all were negative. 2 Nasal swabs samples were collected to rule out Influenza and H1N1.13 serum samples were collected by Weil Felix test out of which 8 samples titration level (OXK) were significantly high. RRT investigated the outbreak. An IEC was conducted along with door to door case search and IEC.

Out of 09 blood samples collected and tested at DPHL Beed; 03 samples were found positive for Dengue (IgM ELISA). Entomological survey done. Source reduction and fogging undertaken in the affected area. Health education given.

Cases reported from Village Lohara Sc Kanegaon, PHC Hipparga Rao, Block Lohara. District RRT investigated the outbreak. District NVBDCP officials were informed. House to house fever survey done. Out of 07 blood samples collected and tested at DPHL Beed; 02 samples were found positive for Dengue (IgM ELISA) and 01 sample was positive for Chikungunya (IgM ELISA). Entomological survey done. Source reduction and fogging undertaken in the affected area. Health education given.

Cases reported from several PHC Mandavgan, Palasdev, Rajgurunagar, Karanjawane. District RRT investigated the outbreak. District NVBDCP officials were informed. House to house fever survey done. Out of 16 blood samples collected and tested at NIV Pune; 14 samples were found positive for Dengue (IgM ELISA). Entomological survey done. Source reduction and fogging undertaken in the affected area. Health education given.

remarks: Cases were reported from Khed, Rajgurunagar, Urban, Rajgurunagar. All 05 serum samples tested positive for Dengue by IgM ELISA. Container survey, Fogging, counselling to patients was done.

Cases reported from Village Belade Bk, SC Belavade, PHC Rethare, Block Karad. Uncovered water storing practices leading to increased density of mosquito larva. 12 serum samples were collected and sent to Civil Lab, out of which 3 were positive for Dengue by IgM ELISA.. House to house survey was done.

remarks: Cases reported from Ketetong, Kujupather, Village/SC Kujupather. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases presented with high-fever, diarrhea and vomiting. Blood samples collected and sent to AMCH, and result positive for Leptospira.

Cases reported from Village Randanga, HSC Sangrampur, CHC Pakur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Two blood samples collected and sent to Institute of Serology Kolkata with the help of WHO Pakur, and both tested negative for Rubella and Measles.

Cases reported from Village/HSC Pangode, PHC Thrikkannapuram, Block Trivandrum Corporation. Vector breeding sites were found in money plant holding containers, flower vases, plastic containers and tyres. 13 Blood samples collected and sent to PH Lab and all samples were found positive.

Cases reported from Ballabhpur (Ward 21 Maulana Chauk) Urban Area, CHC Pakur. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. 4 Blood sample collected and sent to Institute of Serology, Kolkata. All cases treated symptomatically. Vitamin A supplementation provided. Health education given regarding isolation of cases.

Cases reported from Villages Kunnothuparamba and Thripangottur, SC Kallikandy and Cheruparamba, PHC Thripangottur and PHC Kunnothuparamba. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Stool samples collected and sent to GMC Calicut. All cases treated symptomatically. Super chlorination of water sources done. ORS packets were distributed in the community. IEC done regarding safe drinking water and personal hygiene.

Cases reported from Boriwade, SC Boriwade, PHC Borpadale, Block Panhala. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. Out of 18 water sample collected; 5 samples were not potable. Stool sample collected was negative for cholera culture. All cases treated symptomatically. Health education given.

Cases reported from Village/SC Vihe, PHC Malharpeth, Block Patan. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. 5 stool sample collected sent to Civil Lab (sentinel) was negative for cholera culture. All cases treated symptomatically. Health education given.

remarks: Cases reported from Village Belodu, Mandal Rayadurg, and districts Ananthapuram. Cases presented with diarrhea and vomiting. Six water samples collected and sent to IPM Lab Kurnool. State and District RRT investigated the outbreak. Out of the 6 water samples 3 samples were not satisfactory for drinking, found Klebsiella positive (non-potable). Daily House to House survey was done along with ORS distribution, chlorination of water. Cause of outbreak might be consumption of contaminated water. Water pipelines were below the ground level but were open at the endpoint in BC Colony (site of outbreak). A 75-year-old female succumbed to death on 11/05/2023. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral health team on food hygiene, safe drinking water and sanitation.

remarks: Cases were reported from Village: Doasi, Sub-District: Dandkhora, District: Katihar. Cases presented with fever with rash. Affected age group is in between 9 months to 6 years. 5 serum samples were collected and sent to test for Measles, which were found to be positive for Measles by IgM ELISA. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral health team on hygiene, sanitation.

Casesreported from Main Centre Angadipuram S/c area, Angadipuram Panchayath. Cases presented with symptoms of fever, headache, vomiting and abdominal pain. 12 blood samples were sent to DPHL Malapuram which were all found to be positive for Dengue by IgM ELISA. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Health education was given.

Cases reported from ward 13 of Kootttickal Panchayath of Erumley block. Cases presented with symptoms of mild fever, fatigue, loss of appetite, abdominal discomfort and dark colored urine. 7 blood samples were sent to General Hospital Kanjirappally for testing. Among the 7 cases 3 cases were tested positive for Hepatitis A by IgM ELISA. All the affected cases were in the age group of 15 to 24 years. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral health team on hygiene, and sanitation.

remarks: Case was reported from Gigasan Vadi vistar, Taluk Dhari, near Gir Forest. Case admitted to Civil Hospital, Ahmedabad with symptoms of fever, chills and swelling in leg and dark scab on both legs. Blood Sample was sent to Civil Hospital lab, Ahmedabad, which was found to be Positive for Scrub Typhus by IgM ELISA. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral health team on personal and professional hygiene and safe drinking water. House to House survey was done and close contacts were detected during Contact Tracing, all were found to be asymptomatic.

remarks: Cases were reported from various localities under PHC Tikri and CHC Chennai, Sub-District Tikri, and Chenani, District Udhampur. Cases presented with fever, headache and bodyache. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

Total 53 blood samples were collected and sent to RIMS Ranchi. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases were treated symptomatically. 25 out of 53 blood samples came positive for Dengue IgM by ELISA. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

remarks: Cases reported from Gajuwaka, Ward no. 58, SC Colony under the jurisdiction of UPHC Mulagada. Cases presented with symptoms of abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, loose motions and fever with chills after having dinner at Mandi Croods Restaurant in Gajuwaka on 08.10.23 at 9.09 pm. The affected age group was 17 to 28 years. Three water samples were collected from the site & sent to IPM regional lab Visakhapatnam which yielded no inference. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral health team about drinking of boiled cool water and hand washing practices and food hygiene. Awareness was given regrading importance of safe & pure drinking water. The outbreak might be caused by consumption of contaminated food.

district: Ahmedabad, Amc, Arvalli, Banaskanth, Bharuch, Bhavnagar, Chhotaudepur, Dahod, Devbhumi Dwarka, Gandhinagar, Gir Somnath, Gmc, Jamnagar, Jmc, Kheda, Kutch, Mahisagar, Mahesana, Morbi, Narmada, Panch Mahal, Porbandar, Rajkot, Rmc, Sabar Kantha, Smc, Surendranagar ,Vadodara, Vmc
remarks: Cases reported from Districts Districts/Corporations Ahmedabad, AMC, Arvalli, Banaskanth, Bharuch, Bhavnagar, Chhotaudepur, Dahod, Devbhumi Dwarka, Gandhinagar, Gir Somnath, GMC, Jamnagar, JMC, Kheda, Kutch, Mahisagar, Mahesana, Morbi, Narmada, Panch Mahal, Porbandar, Rajkot, RMC, Sabar Kantha, SMC, Surendranagar, Vadodara and VMC. Cases presented with fever, convulsions, altered sensorium. Total 129 serum or CSF samples were sent to NIV Pune and GBRC Gandhinagar. Out of 129 samples, 47 were positive for CHPV by RT-PCR/CHPV Antibody. District RRTs investigated the outbreak. IEC, community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation. Malathion dusting & IRS spraying was done. Surveillance activity was initiated. Total 56 deaths between the age group of 1-15 years have occurred due to the illness, out of which 17 were positive for CHPV.

remarks: Cases were reported from Village Vatluru Sub-District Pedapadu, District Eluru. Cases presented with loose motion and vomiting. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases treated symptomatically. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

Cases were reported from all the 5 BPHCs in Dhemaji district. Cases presented with fever, headache, body pain and altered sensorium. A total of 30 CSF samples and 2 serum samples were collected and tested at AMCH, DCH Dhemaji, and a private lab. 12 samples found positive for JE. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases treated at hospital. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation. A 3-year-old female, 15-year-old female and a 5-year-old male expired due to illness on 30th June, 2025, 9th July, 2025 and 12th July, 2025 repectively.
